n17_partial_closure.lt_cmp_lowered: FAIL — still emits '// unsupported binop: <' because _nvptx_cmp_operand_type_suffix sees both operands classified as F64 (the dst-kind propagation from to_i64 to the binop's operands is missing); kind-aware setp.lt.s64 is never selected
n17_partial_closure.bounds_check_br_cond: FAIL — predicate dst is in %fd<n> (F64) bank instead of %p<n> (.pred), because the unselected setp branch leaves the dst Local default-classified F64; @%fd<n> bra is not valid PTX

ptxas_jit_errors: line 30: Arguments mismatch for instruction 'mul' (mul.f64 with u32 source operands %r4 %r5)
line 32: Arguments mismatch for instruction 'add' (add.f64 with f64 + u32 source operands)
line 34: Arguments mismatch for instruction 'cvt' (cvt.s64.s32 with f64 source operand %fd9)
line 36: Predicate expression expected (@%fd11 bra; %fd11 is FP64, not .pred)
line 39-43: cascading Arguments mismatch for cvt + mov in the if-true block (same F64-default classifier bug for the load/store path operands)

line 45: Parsing error near '-': syntax error (mov.f64 %fd-1, %fd2 — negative register id; %fd-1 means STMT_STORE's dst is _miss sentinel local_id=-1 which is rendered into the register text as `%fd-1`)

scope_diagnosis.root_cause_category: param-type-propagation gap (called out by the task scope as expected blocker)
scope_diagnosis.what_n17_closed: Surface-level 'unsupported call: to_i64' marker — the to_i64 dst is now correctly classified U64 and lowers to cvt.s64.s32
what_n17_did_NOT_close: GPU intrinsic dst type propagation — gpu_block_id_x() returns U32 per gpu/SPEC.md §5 and the dst Local IS classified U32 (visible in `.reg .u32 %r4`), but downstream Locals consuming that U32 via `*` and `+` are default-classified F64 (mul.f64 with u32 sources)
Binop dst-kind inference from operand kinds — when both operands are U32, the binop dst should also be U32 (or at least an integer bank)
_nvptx_cmp_operand_type_suffix tracking — both operands resolve to F64 because the upstream binop chain misclassifies their producers
STMT_STORE _miss sentinel (local_id = -1) emits a negative register id `%fd-1` — separate textual emit bug unrelated to N17
Parameter-type propagation from `.param .u64 my_test_kernel_param_3` to the local that gets the i64 value of `n` — same kind-propagation root cause
scope_diagnosis.honest_scope_marker_quote: From task scope: 'could be the param-type-propagation gap from N17's report (`n: i64` defaults to F64 bank → `setp.lt.s64 %p, %rd1, %fd3` mixed-bank may not JIT)'. CONFIRMED — the gap is even wider than predicted: not just `n` but every Local that consumes a U32 intrinsic result or a `to_i64` cast defaults to F64.

next_cycle_minimal_fix_sketch.step_1: Extend `_nvptx_classify_locals` to perform dst-kind inference: STMT_BINOP dst inherits the kind of its operands when they agree; STMT_ASSIGN dst inherits the source operand kind (instead of always defaulting to F64).
next_cycle_minimal_fix_sketch.step_2: Implement kind-aware binop mnemonic for U32/U64 — `mul.lo.s32`, `add.s32`, etc. (currently only FP variants exist in _nvptx_binop_mnemonic).
next_cycle_minimal_fix_sketch.step_3: Fix the _miss sentinel rendering — STMT_STORE with dst.id == -1 (void store) should emit `st.global.f64 [<addr>], <src>` not `mov.f64 %fd-1, <src>`.
next_cycle_minimal_fix_sketch.step_4: Param-Local kind inference from `.param .u64` declaration — currently the params are declared U64 in the signature but the consuming Locals see them as F64 because the call-into-kernel arg-bind step doesn't tag them.
